wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Imposter Game project overview. Explain the game concept as a real-time multiplayer social deduction game inspired by Among Us, where one player is secretly the imposter while others know the topic. Document the complete game flow including room creation, player management, role assignment, clue submission, discussion, voting, and scoring system. Describe the technology stack (React 18 + Vite + Tailwind CSS for frontend, Node.js + Express + Socket.IO for backend) and the real-time communication architecture. Include the complete scoring system, game phases with timing, and deployment options. Provide both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical implementation details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples demonstrating common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application structure of the Imposter Game frontend. Document the main App.jsx component architecture including screen routing with dynamic component loading, phase-based navigation, and smooth transition animations. Explain the screen mapping system using the screens object, component lifecycle management during phase transitions, and the overlay system for toasts and connection indicators. Detail the main.jsx entry point configuration, React application bootstrap process, and Vite integration. Include practical examples of component composition patterns, state synchronization between screens, and performance optimization techniques for smooth transitions. Address responsive design considerations using CSS custom properties and the dvh viewport unit for mobile-first development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Setup and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the server setup and configuration of the Imposter Game backend. Document the Express server initialization process, Socket.IO configuration with CORS settings, and environment variable handling. Explain the HTTP server creation, middleware setup including CORS and JSON parsing, and the Socket.IO server configuration with origin policies. Detail the health check endpoint implementation and server startup procedures. Include practical examples of server configuration options, security considerations for production deployment, and troubleshooting common server startup issues. Address performance tuning, memory management for in-memory state, and scaling considerations for multiple concurrent rooms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Home Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Home screen component. Detail the room creation and joining interface, form validation and error handling mechanisms, connection status indicators, and user interaction patterns. Document the component's props, state management, and integration with the global game context. Include code examples showing room creation workflow, join room functionality, and error message handling. Explain the responsive design implementation, accessibility features, and visual feedback systems. Address the socket connection status display and connection retry logic. Provide guidelines for customizing the component's appearance and behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed server deployment documentation for the Imposter Game Node.js backend. Document the complete deployment process using Railway platform, including repository setup, environment variable configuration, and production build optimization. Explain the server architecture requirements, port configuration, and CORS settings needed for production. Detail the deployment workflow from GitHub repository to Railway, including required environment variables, database connections, and scaling considerations. Document the server startup process, health check endpoints, and monitoring setup. Include troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues such as port conflicts, memory limits, and connection problems. Address security considerations including HTTPS enforcement, rate limiting, and DDoS protection. Provide guidance on backup strategies, log management, and performance monitoring for the production server.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Imposter Game. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, npm), step-by-step installation instructions for both server and client dependencies, development environment setup, and local server/client startup procedures. Document the complete development workflow including terminal commands, port configurations, and accessing the application locally. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment configuration. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation and explain the expected behavior during development mode. Make this accessible to developers with varying experience levels while providing sufficient technical depth for successful project setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management with GameContext
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the centralized state management system using GameContext.jsx. Explain the context provider architecture, action dispatcher pattern, and reducer-based state updates. Document the complete state structure including game phase tracking, player information, room state, and real-time data synchronization. Detail the action types and their implementations, state mutation patterns, and how components consume context through hooks. Include practical examples of state updates during socket events, error handling in state operations, and performance considerations for frequent state changes. Address the relationship between context consumers and the overall game flow, including how state changes trigger UI updates and screen transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the GameManager class architecture and game state management system. Document the room lifecycle management including room creation, player joining/leaving, and room deletion. Explain the player state tracking mechanism with connection status, role assignments, and score management. Detail the game phase transitions system including clue submission, discussion, voting, and results phases. Document the timer management system with automatic phase progression and manual advancement triggers. Include the serialization/deserialization of game state for client communication, error handling strategies, and state validation patterns. Provide practical examples of room operations, player management, and state synchronization across clients.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lobby Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Lobby screen component. Document the player list management system, host controls and game start functionality, room code display, and player state tracking. Explain the component's role-based UI updates (host vs non-host), player removal functionality, and game initiation process. Include code examples showing player list rendering, host-only controls, and room code presentation. Detail the responsive layout, player status indicators, and interactive elements. Address the integration with game context for real-time updates and the socket event handling for lobby state changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive client deployment documentation for the Imposter Game React frontend. Document the complete deployment process using Vercel platform, including repository setup, build configuration, and environment variable management. Explain the Vite build process optimization, asset bundling, and static site generation configuration. Detail the client-side routing setup, CDN configuration, and performance optimization techniques including code splitting and lazy loading. Document the deployment workflow from GitHub repository to Vercel, including custom domain setup, SSL certificate management, and redirect configurations. Include troubleshooting guidance for common client deployment issues such as build failures, routing problems, and asset loading errors. Address security considerations including Content Security Policy, HTTPS enforcement, and cross-origin resource sharing. Provide guidance on monitoring client-side performance, error tracking, and user experience optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Imposter Game system. Describe the high-level client-server architecture with real-time communication using Socket.IO. Document the component interactions between React frontend and Node.js backend, data flows between components, and integration patterns. Explain the technical decisions behind the event-driven architecture, state management approach using React Context, and the observer pattern implementation. Include system context diagrams showing the relationship between client screens, game context, socket connections, and server-side game manager. Address cross-cutting concerns like real-time synchronization, state persistence, and error handling. Document the technology stack choices, architectural patterns used, and scalability considerations for the current implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Socket Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the socket connection management system implemented in useSocket.js hook. Document the Socket.IO client setup, automatic reconnection logic with exponential backoff, and connection status monitoring. Explain the event handling patterns, message serialization/deserialization, and error recovery mechanisms. Detail the integration with GameContext for seamless state synchronization, graceful disconnection handling, and reconnection state restoration. Include practical examples of socket event listeners, connection lifecycle management, and debugging techniques for real-time communication issues. Address performance optimization for frequent socket operations, memory leak prevention, and best practices for maintaining reliable connections in production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Socket Event System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Socket.IO event system and real-time communication patterns. Document all client-server event mappings including createRoom, joinRoom, startGame, submitClue, submitVote, and imposterGuess events. Explain the event payload structures, parameter validation, and response patterns. Detail the bidirectional communication flow between clients and server, including private vs broadcast events. Document the error handling and validation strategies, callback patterns, and graceful degradation for failed operations. Include practical examples of event handling, state broadcasting, and real-time synchronization patterns. Address connection management, reconnection handling, and graceful disconnection procedures with 30-second grace period implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role Reveal Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Role Reveal screen component. Detail the secret role revelation mechanism, animation and visual feedback systems, and transition to the next game phase. Document the component's role-based rendering (imposter vs crewmate), celebratory effects, and timing controls. Include code examples showing role display logic, animation triggers, and phase progression. Explain the visual design patterns, color schemes for different roles, and celebratory confetti effects. Address the integration with game context for role data and the automatic progression to clue submission phase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create production considerations documentation for the Imposter Game deployment environment. Document performance optimization strategies including WebSocket connection management, memory usage optimization, and CPU resource allocation. Explain scaling considerations for handling multiple concurrent game rooms, player connections, and real-time communication overhead. Detail monitoring and observability setup including server metrics, client analytics, and error tracking systems. Document security hardening measures including input validation, rate limiting, WebSocket security, and DDoS mitigation strategies. Address high availability setup including load balancing, failover mechanisms, and disaster recovery procedures. Include guidance on cost optimization, resource planning, and capacity management for production environments. Document maintenance procedures including automated updates, backup strategies, and emergency response protocols. Provide troubleshooting frameworks for production issues, performance bottlenecks, and user experience problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive server implementation documentation for the Imposter Game backend. Document the Express server setup with Socket.IO integration, CORS configuration, and environment variable handling. Explain the GameManager class architecture including room lifecycle management, player state tracking, and game phase transitions. Detail the Socket.IO event system covering all client-server communication patterns, data payload structures, and error handling strategies. Document the game logic implementation including role assignment algorithms, scoring system mechanics, voting procedures, and timer management. Include the topic categories system with word pack management and theme selection. Provide practical examples of server-side event handling, state synchronization patterns, and real-time communication protocols. Address performance considerations, memory management for in-memory state, and scaling implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Logic Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core game logic implementation including role assignment algorithms, scoring system mechanics, and voting procedures. Document the topic categories system with word pack management for general, family, and adult themes. Explain the role assignment algorithm that randomly selects an imposter among players, and the scoring system with different point values for correct guesses and eliminations. Detail the voting system with tie-breaking mechanisms, vote counting algorithms, and elimination procedures. Document the timer management system with phase-specific durations and automatic progression. Include the imposter guessing functionality, topic reveal mechanics, and round progression logic. Provide practical examples of game flow implementation, state transitions, and scoring calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clue Phase Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Clue Phase screen component. Document the timer display and countdown mechanics, clue submission interface, and clue visualization system. Explain the component's state management for timer updates, clue input handling, and real-time clue display. Include code examples showing timer integration, clue submission validation, and clue rendering. Detail the responsive timer display, input field behavior, and visual feedback for submissions. Address the integration with game context for timer data and the socket events for clue updates. Provide guidelines for customizing the timer appearance and clue display styles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive client implementation documentation for the React-based frontend of the Imposter Game. Document the React application structure including component hierarchy, screen routing with App.jsx, and state management using GameContext.jsx. Explain the centralized state architecture with context providers, action dispatchers, and reducer patterns. Detail the socket connection management through useSocket.js hook including reconnection logic, connection status handling, and error recovery. Document the Vite build configuration, Tailwind CSS styling approach, and responsive design implementation. Include practical examples of component interactions, state updates, and real-time synchronization patterns. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management, and user experience considerations for the mobile-first design approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Discussion Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Discussion screen component. Detail the clue review and sharing interface, timer management during discussions, and discussion interaction patterns. Document the component's role in facilitating player communication, clue analysis, and debate coordination. Include code examples showing clue display logic, timer integration, and discussion state management. Explain the responsive layout for clue presentation, timer visualization, and user interaction elements. Address the integration with game context for clue data and the socket events for discussion updates. Provide guidelines for customizing the discussion interface and clue presentation styles.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed UI component documentation for all game screens in the Imposter Game. Document each screen component including Home (room creation/joining), Lobby (player management), RoleReveal (secret role display), CluePhase (timer-based clue submission), Discussion (clue review and debate), Voting (target selection), and Results (scoreboard and celebrations). For each component, describe the visual appearance, user interaction patterns, prop requirements, state management, and integration with the global game context. Include usage examples with code snippets, responsive design considerations, and accessibility compliance. Document component animations, transitions, and visual feedback systems. Address cross-component communication patterns and data flow between screens. Provide guidelines for component customization and theming support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voting Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Voting screen component. Document the vote casting interface, target selection and confirmation system, and voting animation effects. Explain the component's role in collecting player votes, validating selections, and handling voting outcomes. Include code examples showing target selection logic, vote submission handling, and visual feedback for selections. Detail the responsive voting interface, target highlighting, and confirmation animations. Address the integration with game context for player data and the socket events for vote collection. Provide guidelines for customizing the voting interface and selection feedback animations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Rules and Scoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Imposter Game rules and scoring system. Detail the complete game flow including room management (4-8 players), role assignment with secret imposter designation, and the five distinct phases: clue submission (60s), discussion (60s), voting, role reveal, and results display. Document the scoring system with point allocations (+2 for correct votes, +3 for surviving imposter, +1 consolation), tie-breaking mechanisms, and round progression through three total rounds. Explain the topic categories (General, Family, Adult) with content guidelines and randomization processes. Include phase-specific rules, timing management, and automatic progression logic. Provide practical examples of game scenarios, scoring calculations, and edge cases. Address fairness considerations, game balance, and strategic gameplay elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Results Screen
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Results screen component. Detail the round result display, scoreboard and standings presentation, and celebration animation systems. Document the component's role in showing voting outcomes, score updates, and game progression indicators. Include code examples showing score calculation, leaderboard rendering, and celebration effects. Explain the responsive scoreboard layout, ranking display, and visual feedback for winners. Address the integration with game context for score data and the socket events for result updates. Provide guidelines for customizing the results display and celebration animations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Production
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for the Imposter Game production environment. Document the complete deployment process for both server and client applications including Railway deployment for the Node.js server and Vercel deployment for the React client. Detail environment variable configuration, build optimization settings, and production-specific considerations. Explain the deployment workflow from GitHub repository to production environments, including required configuration steps, domain setup, and SSL certificate management. Document scaling considerations, performance optimization techniques, and monitoring approaches for production deployments. Include troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues, rollback procedures, and maintenance operations. Address security considerations, load balancing, and high availability setup for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for contributing to the Imposter Game project. Document code organization principles, file structure conventions, and naming patterns used throughout the codebase. Establish coding standards for both frontend React components and backend Node.js modules, including component architecture, state management patterns, and socket event handling. Detail the testing strategy including unit testing approaches for React components, integration testing for socket communications, and manual testing procedures for real-time gameplay. Document the pull request process, issue reporting guidelines, and code review standards. Include best practices for maintaining real-time synchronization, handling edge cases, and optimizing performance. Address debugging techniques, logging strategies, and development tools recommendations for efficient development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for the Imposter Game. Document common connection issues including Socket.IO reconnection problems, network connectivity failures, and timeout scenarios with their solutions. Address performance optimization techniques for both client and server applications, including memory management, rendering optimization, and real-time communication efficiency. Explain error message interpretations, debugging strategies, and diagnostic tools for identifying and resolving issues. Document known limitations of the current implementation, platform-specific considerations, and browser compatibility requirements. Include step-by-step resolution guides for frequent problems, preventive measures, and best practices for avoiding common pitfalls. Address scalability challenges, production monitoring, and maintenance procedures for long-term operation.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Imposter Game: Real-Time Multiplayer Party Game

## Project Introduction

**Imposter Game** is a real-time multiplayer party game inspired by the popular social deduction game "Among Us". The project provides a complete implementation of the core gameplay mechanics including room-based multiplayer, role assignment, clue submission, discussion phases, and voting systems - all built with modern web technologies.

### Purpose Statement
The game allows 4-8 players to participate in an interactive social deduction experience where one player secretly acts as the imposter trying to eliminate crewmates while the other players attempt to identify and vote out the imposter.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Multiplayer Experience**: Synchronize game state across multiple clients using WebSocket technology
- **Complete Game Flow**: Implement all phases of the classic Imposter game including clue-giving, discussion, and voting
- **Robust State Management**: Handle complex game state transitions and player interactions
- **Responsive UI**: Provide an engaging user interface with smooth animations and feedback
- **Network Resilience**: Support player disconnections with graceful reconnection handling

### Target Audience
- Social gamers seeking interactive party experiences
- Groups looking for entertaining online games during virtual gatherings
- Developers interested in real-time multiplayer game architecture

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a **client-server architecture** with clear separation of concerns between the frontend React application and the backend Node.js server.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[React Frontend]
    B --> C[Socket.IO Client]
    C --> D[Express Server]
    D --> E[Socket.IO Server]
    E --> F[Game Manager]
    F --> G[Room Management]
    F --> H[Player State]
    F --> I[Game Logic]
    
    J[Topics Database] --> F
    K[Configuration] --> D
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend (Client)**
- React-based single-page application with custom hooks
- Real-time communication via Socket.IO client
- State management through React Context API
- Tailwind CSS for styling with custom animations

**Backend (Server)**
- Node.js Express server with Socket.IO for real-time communication
- Centralized game state management
- Room-based architecture for multi-game support
- Persistent session handling for player reconnection

### Design Patterns

1. **Observer Pattern**: Used extensively for real-time event broadcasting
2. **State Machine**: Game phases transition through defined states
3. **Factory Pattern**: Room creation and player management
4. **Command Pattern**: Socket event handlers encapsulate game actions

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Client Browser
    participant Socket as Socket.IO Client
    participant Server as Express Server
    participant Manager as Game Manager
    participant Room as Room Instance
    
    Client->>+Socket: connect()
    Socket->>+Server: WebSocket handshake
    Server->>+Manager: initialize connection
    Manager->>+Room: create room instance
    Room-->>-Manager: room ready
    Manager-->>-Server: room created
    Server-->>-Socket: roomCreated event
    Socket-->>-Client: roomCreated data
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Server Entry Point**: `server/index.js`
- Express server initialization with CORS support
- Socket.IO server setup with real-time communication
- Complete game event handling system
- Graceful error handling and logging

**Client Entry Point**: `client/src/main.jsx`
- React application bootstrap
- Provider setup for game state management
- Root component rendering

### Core Modules

**Game Manager**: `server/gameManager.js`
- Central game state machine managing all rooms and players
- Implements complete game logic including scoring and voting
- Handles room lifecycle and player management
- Provides robust error handling and validation

**Topic Database**: `server/topics.js`
- Organized word categories for game themes
- Three difficulty levels: general, family, adult
- Ensures sufficient variety for engaging gameplay

**Game Context**: `client/src/context/GameContext.jsx`
- Centralized state management for the entire application
- Socket event handling and data synchronization
- User interaction handlers and action dispatchers
- Toast notification system for user feedback

### Configuration Approach

**Client Configuration**: `client/vite.config.js`
- Development server with proxy configuration for Socket.IO
- React plugin integration
- Port configuration for local development

**Environment Variables**: `client/src/hooks/useSocket.js`
- Dynamic server URL configuration
- Reconnection handling with exponential backoff
- Transport protocol selection (WebSocket/Polling)

### External Dependencies

**Server Dependencies**:
- `express`: Web server framework
- `socket.io`: Real-time bidirectional communication
- `cors`: Cross-origin resource sharing support

**Client Dependencies**:
- `react`: UI framework
- `react-dom`: React DOM rendering
- `socket.io-client`: Real-time client communication
- `canvas-confetti`: Visual effects library

### Integration Points

**Socket Communication Flow**:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Action] --> B[Socket Event]
    B --> C[Server Handler]
    C --> D[Game Manager]
    D --> E[Update State]
    E --> F[Broadcast Updates]
    F --> G[All Clients Receive]
    G --> H[UI Updates]
```

**Reconnection System**:
- Automatic reconnection on network interruption
- Session restoration with game state sync
- Graceful handling of temporary disconnections

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Home Screen] --> B[Lobby Screen]
    B --> C[Role Reveal]
    C --> D[Clue Phase]
    D --> E[Discussion]
    E --> F[Voting]
    F --> G[Results]
    G --> H[Next Round]
    H --> C
    
    I[Game Context] --> A
    I --> B
    I --> C
    I --> D
    I --> E
    I --> F
    I --> G
    I --> H
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multiplayer Room System**
- Unique 4-character room codes for easy sharing
- Host-based room management with automatic host transfer
- Player capacity limits (4-8 players)
- Real-time player presence indicators

**Complete Game Flow**
- Role assignment with secret imposter designation
- Timed clue submission phase (60 seconds)
- Discussion phase with clue sharing (60 seconds)
- Voting phase with elimination (45 seconds)
- Round progression and final scoring

**Advanced Game Mechanics**
- Scoring system with different point values for various outcomes
- Tie-breaking mechanisms for votes
- Imposter guessing with consolation points
- Round-based progression with topic rotation

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Communication**: `client/src/hooks/useSocket.js`
- WebSocket-based communication with automatic reconnection
- Event-driven architecture for responsive updates
- Connection status monitoring and user feedback

**State Management**: `client/src/context/GameContext.jsx`
- Comprehensive game state tracking across all components
- Toast notification system for user feedback
- Error handling with user-friendly messages

**Game Logic**: `server/gameManager.js`
- Complete implementation of game rules and scoring
- Robust validation and error handling
- Efficient memory management for multiple concurrent games

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Home
    Home --> Lobby: Create/Join Room
    Lobby --> RoleReveal: Start Game
    RoleReveal --> Clue: Show Topic
    Clue --> Discussion: Time Up
    Discussion --> Voting: Time Up
    Voting --> Results: Time Up
    Results --> RoleReveal: Next Round
    RoleReveal --> FinalResults: Game Over
    FinalResults --> Lobby: Play Again
    Home --> [*]: Leave Game
```

**Scoring System Flow**:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Vote Tally] --> B{Was Imposter?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Correct Voters: +2]
    B -->|No| D[Imposter: +3]
    C --> E[Round Complete]
    D --> E
    E --> F{More Rounds?}
    F -->|Yes| G[Next Round]
    F -->|No| H[Final Results]
```

---

**Sources:**
- [server/index.js](server/index.js)
- [server/gameManager.js](server/gameManager.js)
- [server/topics.js](server/topics.js)
- [client/src/App.jsx](client/src/App.jsx)
- [client/src/context/GameContext.jsx](client/src/context/GameContext.jsx)
- [client/src/hooks/useSocket.js](client/src/hooks/useSocket.js)
- [client/src/screens/Home.jsx](client/src/screens/Home.jsx)
- [client/package.json](client/package.json)
- [server/package.json](server/package.json)
- [client/vite.config.js](client/vite.config.js)

</blog>
